title: 2024 Oscar Nominations: Snubs, Surprises, and WTFs
format: Sean Fennessey and Amanda Dobbins react to the 2024 Oscar nominations the morning they dropped: Oppenheimer's 13, the Gerwig and Robbie snubs, and the year's what-the-fucks.

segments.heading: The Best Picture and Director races

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.note: Led the field with 13 nominations, one shy of the all-time record held by All About Eve, La La Land, and Titanic. Fennessey calls it Oppenheimer's Oscar to lose.

films.title: Poor Things
films.note: Eleven nominations, which the hosts read as the real threat to Oppenheimer in the craft categories. Lanthimos held his Director slot; Mark Ruffalo got in, Willem Dafoe did not.

films.title: Killers of the Flower Moon
films.note: Scorsese's 10th directing nomination, most among living filmmakers. DiCaprio was snubbed and the film missed Adapted Screenplay — Fennessey fears another Irishman situation.

films.title: Barbie
films.note: Eight nominations, but Greta Gerwig missed Best Director and Margot Robbie missed Best Actress. The hosts' central grievance: enough love to keep Robbie and Gerwig on stage, none of the real awards.

films.title: The Zone of Interest
films.note: Five nominations including Director for Jonathan Glazer. Fennessey calls it the least commercial film in the field and a work of textural art more than a movie-movie.

films.title: Anatomy of a Fall
films.note: Justine Triet took a Director slot over Gerwig; Sandra Hüller landed in Best Actress and the film cracked Film Editing, a sign voters take it seriously.

films.title: The Holdovers
films.note: Five nominations, but Alexander Payne missed Director and Dominic Sessa missed Supporting Actor — no splashy surprise anywhere on the morning.

films.title: American Fiction
films.note: Five nominations including Sterling K. Brown in Supporting Actor and an Adapted Screenplay nod.

films.title: Past Lives
films.note: Best Picture plus Original Screenplay for Celine Song — the nomination Dobbins was most anxious about surviving.

films.title: Maestro
films.note: Seven quiet nominations Fennessey found forgettable; the only Best Picture nominee from a streamer as the Netflix-era wave recedes.

films.title: Nyad
films.note: Annette Bening and Jodie Foster both nominated; Fennessey files Bening's under campaign-driven and wildly forgettable.

films.title: Rustin
films.note: Colman Domingo's Best Actor nod, which the hosts read as the slot DiCaprio lost. Traditional biopic work behind a strong Netflix campaign.

films.title: The Color Purple
films.note: Danielle Brooks in a funky Supporting Actress field.

films.title: May December
films.note: Only an Original Screenplay nomination — nothing for Charles Melton, Julianne Moore, or Todd Haynes.

films.title: The Boy and the Heron
films.note: Miyazaki, and the presumptive winner.

films.title: Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse
films.note: In the field, though its score was snubbed — which Fennessey found galling.

films.title: Elemental
films.note: Pixar avoided the shutout the Annie snubs hinted at.

films.title: Robot Dreams
films.note: Pablo Berger's Spanish film, the category's surprise entrant.

films.title: Wish
films.note: Disney's centennial film, shut out despite the hosts' garage full of Wish gear.

films.title: Godzilla Minus One
films.note: Visual Effects nominee and a possible winner; the room reportedly screamed when it was announced.

films.title: The Creator
films.note: Two nominations; Fennessey rates its effects work legit.

films.title: Napoleon
films.note: Three nominations for Ridley Scott.

films.title: Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Part One
films.note: Two nominations, and the launchpad for a long bit about how many emails it took to drop 'Part One' from the title.

films.title: El Conde
films.note: Ed Lachman's Cinematography nod, the happiest surprise of the morning for Fennessey.

films.title: Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny
films.note: John Williams' 54th nomination, for Best Score — which Fennessey found an odd inclusion.

films.title: Society of the Snow
films.note: Netflix's international entry; the hosts wondered whether a fall release would have made it a bigger player.

films.title: The Taste of Things
films.note: France's submission, left out of International Feature — a travesty in Fennessey's view.

films.title: Fallen Leaves
films.note: Also snubbed in International Feature.

films.title: Flamin' Hot
films.note: 'The Fire Inside' nominated for Original Song — the Cheetos movie, barely based on reality.

films.title: American Symphony
films.note: 'It Never Went Away' got a song nod even though the film missed Documentary Feature.

films.title: 20 Days in Mariupol
films.note: The Documentary Feature front-runner, chronicling the devastation in Ukraine.

films.title: The Eternal Memory
films.note: Fennessey's likely runner-up in Documentary — a marriage, Alzheimer's, and a journalist.

films.title: To Kill a Tiger
films.note: A very small release, so its Documentary nomination surprised both hosts.

films.title: The Wonderful Story of Henry Sugar
films.note: Wes Anderson's short, all but certain to win — while Asteroid City got nothing.

films: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
All Quiet on the Western Front
Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Mutant Mayhem
